courseleaf.title: Oceanography, Minor
courseleaf.informationtext:
Oceanography is the study of the earth's oceans, investigating connections between geological, biological, chemical and physical processes in the marine realm, and the interactions between the Earth's ocean/atmosphere system. The interdisciplinary minor in oceanography is for students with backgrounds in any of these fields, as well as those interested in marine policy and conservation. The curriculum reflects the integrative nature of oceanography, with core courses covering the major disciplines in oceanography and elective courses that allow students to cater the minor to their interests. The oceanography minor includes courses taught at the Davis campus and courses offered at Bodega Marine Laboratory.

Students majoring in Geology may elect to complete a minor in Geophysics, Environmental Geology, or Oceanography. They may not complete a minor in Geology.

Students majoring in Marine & Coastal Science may elect to complete a minor in Geology, Geophysics, or Environmental Geology. They may not complete a minor in Oceanography. 

The minor is sponsored by the Department of Earth & Planetary Sciences.

Declare your minor using the OASIS Minor Declaration form, due the quarter before graduation. 

Work with your major advisor and college advisors to fit a minor into your overall academic plan. 

Advising

Visit the staff major advisor for help navigating minor requirements, policies, prerequisites, and course offerings. Visit the faculty major advisors for additional advice on courses, careers, and graduate school.

Faculty Advisor

T. M. Hill.
courseleaf.requirementstext:
Course List
Code Title Units
GEL 116N/ESP 116N DISCONTINUED FOR FALL 2026Oceanography (Discontinued for fall 2026) **3
or EPS 116 Oceanography
Choose two:7-8
GEL/ESP/EPS 150A
Physical & Chemical Oceanography (GEL 150A Discontinued for fall 2026) **
GEL 150B/ESP 150B DISCONTINUED FOR FALL 2026 **
Geological Oceanography (Discontinued for fall 2026) **
or EPS 150B
 Geological Oceanography
GEL 150C/ESP 150C DISCONTINUED FOR FALL 2026 **
Biological Oceanography (GEL 150C Discontinued for fall 2026) *, **
or EPS 150C
 Biological Oceanography
Choose additional courses from above and/or from the following list to reach at least 18 total units for the minor:8
ATM 121A
Atmospheric Dynamics
ATM 121B
Atmospheric Dynamics
ATM 158
Boundary-Layer Meteorology
ESP 124
Marine & Coastal Field Ecology *
ESP 152
Coastal Oceanography *
ETX 120
Perspectives in Aquatic Toxicology
ETX/NUT 127
Environmental Stress & Development in Marine Organisms *
EVE 106
 (Discontinued for winter 2026) *, **
EVE 112
Biology of Invertebrates
EVE 114
Experimental Invertebrate Biology *
EVE 115
Marine Ecology
GEL 108
Earth History: Paleoclimates (Discontinued for fall 2026) **
or EPS 108
 Paleoclimatology
GEL 109
Earth History: Sediments & Strata (Discontinued for fall 2026) **
or EPS 109
 Sediments & Strata
GEL 152
 (Discontinued for winter 2026) **
WFC 120
Biology & Conservation of Fishes
WFC 157
Coastal Ecosystems
Total Units18-19
*

Course taught at Bodega Marine Laboratory.

**

Course(s) discontinued; see your advisor for course options.
courseleaf.quickviewtext: The Oceanography minor studies the Earth’s oceans, investigating connections between geological, biological, chemical, and physical processes and the interactions between the Earth’s ocean/atmosphere system. Students study the integrative nature of oceanography, with elective courses that cater the minor to their interests.
